Value: Solder-flux contamination forms during electronics soldering operations, as rosin-based and no-clean fluxes deposit resinous residues on substrates. This layer, it adheres tenaciously to copper and brass, which distinguishes it from water-soluble types by its non-volatile, sticky persistence that manifests under exposure. On PCB surfaces, the contamination exhibits strong bonding dependent from humidity, posing removal challenges like chemical resistance, yet laser cleaning effectively ablates it precisely, without damaging the underlying materials.
